# OnlyNative UI ‚Äî Full API Reference

> Design-system agnostic component library for React Native ‚Äî ships with Material Design 3
> Packages: `@onlynative/core`, `@onlynative/components`
> Requirements: React Native 0.81+, React 19+, Expo SDK 54+
> Peer deps: `react-native-safe-area-context >=4`
> Optional peer deps: `@expo/vector-icons >=14` (only needed for icon props)

---

## Quick Start (new project)

```bash
npx onlynative create my-app
cd my-app
npx expo start
```

The `create` command scaffolds a ready-to-run Expo project with `ThemeProvider`, example components (Button, Card), Expo Router, and all dependencies pre-configured.

Interactive prompts: project name, display name (shown on home screen), package manager (npm/yarn/pnpm/bun), install dependencies.

Options:
- `-y, --yes` ‚Äî Skip all prompts, use defaults (name: `my-app`, pm: `npm`, auto-install)

Pass name directly: `npx onlynative create my-app`

## Installation (existing project)

```bash
pnpm add @onlynative/core @onlynative/components @expo/vector-icons react-native-safe-area-context
```

---

## CLI (`onlynative`)

### Commands

#### `onlynative create [name]`

Create a new project with OnlyNative UI pre-configured. Fetches the quickstart template, applies your project name to `package.json` and `app.json`, and optionally installs dependencies.

```bash
npx onlynative create            # Interactive
npx onlynative create my-app     # With name
npx onlynative create my-app -y  # Non-interactive, accept defaults
```

Options:
- `-y, --yes` ‚Äî Skip prompts and use defaults
- `-t, --template <name>` ‚Äî Template to use (`blank`, `with-router`)
- `--package-manager <pm>` ‚Äî Package manager to use (`npm`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun`)

#### `onlynative init`

Copy-paste workflow ‚Äî copies component source files into your project. The theme system (`@onlynative/core`) stays as an npm dependency.

Initialize project. Detects project type (Expo/RN), package manager, and tsconfig path aliases.

```bash
npx onlynative init            # Interactive
npx onlynative init -y         # Non-interactive, accept defaults
npx onlynative init -y --components-alias "~/ui" --lib-alias "~/utils"
```

Options:
- `-y, --yes` ‚Äî Skip all prompts, use detected defaults. Overwrites existing config. Auto-installs `@onlynative/core`.
- `--components-alias <alias>` ‚Äî Components install path. Default: `@/components/ui` (or `~/components/ui` if `~/*` alias detected in tsconfig)
- `--lib-alias <alias>` ‚Äî Utility files path. Default: `@/lib` (or `~/lib` if `~/*` alias detected)
- `--package-manager <pm>` ‚Äî Package manager to use (`npm`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun`)

Creates `onlynative.json`:
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://onlynative.dev/schema.json",
  "aliases": { "components": "@/components/ui", "lib": "@/lib" },
  "registryUrl": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/onlynative/ui",
  "registryVersion": "main"
}
```

#### `onlynative add <components...>`

Add components to your project. Resolves dependency graph, copies files with rewritten imports, installs npm deps.

```bash
npx onlynative add button
npx onlynative add card chip text-field
npx onlynative add appbar      # auto-adds icon-button + typography
```

Options:
- `-f, --force` ‚Äî Overwrite existing components
- `-d, --dry-run` ‚Äî Preview without writing files
- `--package-manager <pm>` ‚Äî Package manager to use (`npm`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun`)

#### `onlynative update [components...]`

Update installed components to latest registry version.

Options:
- `-a, --all` ‚Äî Update all installed components
- `-d, --dry-run` ‚Äî Show diff without applying

#### `onlynative upgrade`

Upgrade `@onlynative/core` to the latest published version and install any new peer dependencies.

```bash
npx onlynative upgrade         # Interactive ‚Äî shows plan and prompts before installing
npx onlynative upgrade -y      # Non-interactive ‚Äî skip confirmation
```

Options:
- `-y, --yes` ‚Äî Skip confirmation prompt
- `--package-manager <pm>` ‚Äî Package manager to use (`npm`, `yarn`, `pnpm`, `bun`)

What it does:
1. Reads the installed `@onlynative/core` version from `node_modules`
2. Fetches the latest version from the npm registry
3. Compares peer dependencies between the installed and latest versions
4. Shows a plan: version bump, new required peer deps, changed version ranges, removed deps
5. Upgrades `@onlynative/core` and installs any new required peer dependencies in one step
6. Reports optional peer deps that aren't installed (does not auto-install optional deps)
7. Lists peer deps that are no longer required so you can remove them manually

#### `onlynative list`

Show available components with install status.

#### `onlynative doctor`

Check project health: config validity, core installation, RN version, file integrity, peer deps.

---

## @onlynative/utils

Shared utilities used by `@onlynative/components` and available for custom component development.

```tsx
import { alphaColor, blendColor, elevationStyle, getMaterialCommunityIcons, transformOrigin, selectRTL } from '@onlynative/utils'
```

### Color helpers

- `alphaColor(color: string, alpha: number): string` ‚Äî Converts hex color to `rgba(...)` with the given alpha (clamped 0‚Äì1). Returns the input unchanged if parsing fails.
- `blendColor(base: string, overlay: string, overlayAlpha: number): string` ‚Äî Blends two hex colors by mixing RGB channels at the given overlay opacity. Returns `rgb(...)`.

### Elevation

- `elevationStyle(level: ElevationLevel): ViewStyle` ‚Äî Converts an MD3 elevation level into platform-appropriate shadow styles. Uses `boxShadow` on web, `shadow*` + `elevation` on native.

> **Gotcha:** the return shape differs by platform. Both are typed as `ViewStyle`, but spreading the result and overriding individual `shadow*` props silently no-ops on web ‚Äî the shadow is baked into the `boxShadow` string. To customize, modify the `ElevationLevel` before calling, or branch on `Platform.OS`.

### RTL support

- `transformOrigin(vertical?: 'top' | 'center' | 'bottom'): string` ‚Äî Returns `"left top"` or `"right top"` based on RTL layout direction. Used for label animations.
- `selectRTL<T>(ltr: T, rtl: T): T` ‚Äî Picks a value based on layout direction.

### Icon resolver

- `getMaterialCommunityIcons()` ‚Äî Lazily resolves `MaterialCommunityIcons` from `@expo/vector-icons` at render time. Throws with install instructions if the package is missing.

### Test helper (subpath export)

```tsx
import { renderWithTheme } from '@onlynative/utils/test'
```

- `renderWithTheme(ui: ReactElement, options?: RenderOptions)` ‚Äî Wraps `@testing-library/react-native`'s `render` with `ThemeProvider`.

---

## @onlynative/core

### ThemeProvider

Wrap your app root to supply the theme to all components. Works with any design system ‚Äî Material Design 3 or custom themes. Defaults to the MD3 light theme when no theme is provided.

```tsx
import { ThemeProvider, darkTheme } from '@onlynative/core'

// Light theme (default)
<ThemeProvider>{children}</ThemeProvider>

// Dark theme
<ThemeProvider theme={darkTheme}>{children}</ThemeProvider>

// Custom theme
import { lightTheme } from '@onlynative/core'
import type { Theme } from '@onlynative/core'

const custom: Theme = {
  ...lightTheme,
  colors: { ...lightTheme.colors, primary: '#006A6A', onPrimary: '#FFFFFF' },
}
<ThemeProvider theme={custom}>{children}</ThemeProvider>
```

Props:
- `theme?: BaseTheme` ‚Äî Theme object. Default: `lightTheme` (MD3)
- `children: ReactNode`

### useTheme()

Returns the current theme from the nearest `ThemeProvider`.

Without a type parameter, returns the Material Design 3 `Theme`. Pass a custom theme type for typed access to your design system's tokens.

```tsx
import { useTheme } from '@onlynative/core'

// Material Design 3 (default)
const theme = useTheme()
// theme.colors.primary, theme.typography.bodyMedium, theme.spacing.md, etc.

// Custom design system
const theme = useTheme<MyTheme>()
// theme.colors.brand, theme.typography.heading, etc.
```

### defineTheme(theme)

Identity function that validates a custom theme object against `BaseTheme`. Provides type-checking and autocompletion.

```tsx
import { defineTheme } from '@onlynative/core'
import type { BaseTheme, TextStyle } from '@onlynative/core'

interface MyColors { [key: string]: string; brand: string; background: string; text: string }
interface MyTypography { [key: string]: TextStyle; heading: TextStyle; body: TextStyle }
interface MyTheme extends BaseTheme { colors: MyColors; typography: MyTypography }

const myTheme = defineTheme<MyTheme>({
  colors: { brand: '#FF6B00', background: '#FFFFFF', text: '#1A1A1A' },
  typography: {
    heading: { fontFamily: 'Inter', fontSize: 24, fontWeight: '700', lineHeight: 32, letterSpacing: 0 },
    body: { fontFamily: 'Inter', fontSize: 16, fontWeight: '400', lineHeight: 24, letterSpacing: 0.5 },
  },
  shape: { roundness: 1, cornerNone: 0, cornerExtraSmall: 4, cornerSmall: 8, cornerMedium: 12, cornerLarge: 16, cornerExtraLarge: 28, cornerFull: 9999 },
  spacing: { xs: 4, sm: 8, md: 16, lg: 24, xl: 32 },
  stateLayer: { pressedOpacity: 0.12, focusedOpacity: 0.12, hoveredOpacity: 0.08, disabledOpacity: 0.38 },
  elevation: { ... },
  motion: { ... },
})
```

### createMaterialTheme(seedColor, options?)

Generates a complete MD3 light and dark theme from a single seed color. Uses Google's HCT color space via `@material/material-color-utilities` for spec-compliant palette generation.

**Options:**
- `fontFamily?: string` ‚Äî Custom font family applied to all 15 typography styles. When omitted, platform defaults are used (Roboto on Android, System on iOS).
- `roundness?: number` ‚Äî Global corner-radius multiplier. `0` = sharp corners, `1` = default MD3 (default), `2` = double rounding. Does not affect `cornerNone` or `cornerFull`.

**Separate entry point** ‚Äî keeps the ~60 kB dependency out of the main bundle:

```tsx
import { createMaterialTheme } from '@onlynative/core/create-theme'
import { ThemeProvider } from '@onlynative/core'

const { lightTheme, darkTheme } = createMaterialTheme('#006A6A')

// Custom font
const { lightTheme, darkTheme } = createMaterialTheme('#006A6A', { fontFamily: 'Inter' })

// Sharp corners
const { lightTheme, darkTheme } = createMaterialTheme('#006A6A', { roundness: 0 })

// Use in provider
<ThemeProvider theme={lightTheme}>{children}</ThemeProvider>

// Or with dark mode
<ThemeProvider theme={isDark ? darkTheme : lightTheme}>{children}</ThemeProvider>
```

Returns: `{ lightTheme: Theme, darkTheme: Theme }`

The generated themes include all 69 MD3 color roles plus shared tokens (typography, shape, spacing, stateLayer, elevation, motion, topAppBar).

Requires `@material/material-color-utilities` as a peer dependency: `npm install @material/material-color-utilities`

### applyRoundness(roundness)

Scales the MD3 corner radius tokens by a multiplier. Returns a complete `Shape` object. `cornerNone` (0) and `cornerFull` (999) are never affected.

- `roundness: 0` ‚Äî sharp corners (all intermediate tokens become 0)
- `roundness: 1` ‚Äî default MD3 values
- `roundness: 2` ‚Äî double the rounding

```tsx
import { applyRoundness, lightTheme } from '@onlynative/core'

// Use with spread to override shape on an existing theme
const sharpTheme = { ...lightTheme, shape: applyRoundness(0) }

// Or with defineTheme
import { defineTheme } from '@onlynative/core'
const theme = defineTheme({ ...lightTheme, shape: applyRoundness(0.5) })
```

### material preset

Grouped object containing all Material Design 3 theme values.

```tsx
import { material } from '@onlynative/core'

material.lightTheme  // MD3 light theme
material.darkTheme   // MD3 dark theme
material.defaultTopAppBarTokens
```

### Theme type hierarchy

- `BaseTheme` ‚Äî Generic base interface. All design systems extend this. Has `colors: Record<string, string>`, `typography: Record<string, TextStyle>`, plus shape, spacing, stateLayer, elevation, motion.
- `Theme` ‚Äî Material Design 3 theme. Extends `BaseTheme` with 69 MD3 color roles, 15 typography variants, and optional `topAppBar` tokens. `MaterialTheme` is an identical alias (same type) ‚Äî use it to disambiguate in multi-design-system codebases.

### Theme structure

```
BaseTheme {
  colors: Record<string, string>
  typography: Record<string, TextStyle>
  shape: Shape           ‚Äî roundness, cornerNone, cornerExtraSmall, cornerSmall, cornerMedium, cornerLarge, cornerExtraLarge, cornerFull
  spacing: Spacing       ‚Äî xs, sm, md, lg, xl
  elevation: Elevation   ‚Äî level0..level3 (shadow properties)
  stateLayer: StateLayer ‚Äî pressedOpacity, focusedOpacity, hoveredOpacity, disabledOpacity
  motion: Motion         ‚Äî duration and easing tokens
}

Theme extends BaseTheme {
  colors: Colors         ‚Äî 69 MD3 color roles
  typography: Typography ‚Äî 15 type scale variants (displayLarge..labelSmall)
  topAppBar?: TopAppBarTokens
}
```

Colors: primary, onPrimary, primaryContainer, onPrimaryContainer, primaryFixed, onPrimaryFixed, primaryFixedDim, onPrimaryFixedVariant, secondary (same pattern), tertiary (same pattern), error, onError, errorContainer, onErrorContainer, background, onBackground, surface, surfaceDim, surfaceBright, surfaceContainerLowest, surfaceContainerLow, surfaceContainer, surfaceContainerHigh, surfaceContainerHighest, onSurface, surfaceVariant, onSurfaceVariant, outline, outlineVariant, surfaceTint, shadow, scrim, inverseSurface, inverseOnSurface, inversePrimary

Typography variants: displayLarge, displayMedium, displaySmall, headlineLarge, headlineMedium, headlineSmall, titleLarge, titleMedium, titleSmall, bodyLarge, bodyMedium, bodySmall, labelLarge, labelMedium, labelSmall ‚Äî each with fontFamily, fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, letterSpacing

### useBreakpoint()

Returns the current MD3 window size class. Reactively updates on resize.

```tsx
import { useBreakpoint } from '@onlynative/core'

const bp = useBreakpoint()
// 'compact' (0-599) | 'medium' (600-839) | 'expanded' (840-1199) | 'large' (1200-1599) | 'extraLarge' (1600+)
```

### useBreakpointValue(values)

Returns a value based on the current breakpoint with cascade fallback.

```tsx
import { useBreakpointValue } from '@onlynative/core'

const columns = useBreakpointValue({ compact: 1, medium: 2, expanded: 4 })
// compact ‚Üí 1, medium ‚Üí 2, expanded/large/extraLarge ‚Üí 4
```

Type: `useBreakpointValue<T>(values: Partial<Record<Breakpoint, T>> & Record<'compact', T>): T`

---

## @onlynative/components

Import via subpath (preferred): `import { X } from '@onlynative/components/x'`
Import via root: `import { X } from '@onlynative/components'`

### Component override pattern

All interactive components follow a 3-tier override system. Merge order: theme defaults ‚Üí variant ‚Üí semantic props ‚Üí style props (last wins).

Standard override props on interactive components:
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Root container background. Hover/press state-layer colors auto-derived.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Content (label + icons) color. State-layer colors auto-derived.
- `labelStyle?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Text-specific overrides (does not affect icons).
- `style` ‚Äî Root container style.

Disabled state always uses standard MD3 disabled treatment (38% onSurface) regardless of overrides.

---

### Typography

```tsx
import { Typography } from '@onlynative/components/typography'

<Typography variant="headlineMedium" color="#333">Hello</Typography>
```

Props:
- `children: ReactNode` ‚Äî Content to display. Accepts strings, numbers, or nested elements.
- `variant?: 'displayLarge' | 'displayMedium' | 'displaySmall' | 'headlineLarge' | 'headlineMedium' | 'headlineSmall' | 'titleLarge' | 'titleMedium' | 'titleSmall' | 'bodyLarge' | 'bodyMedium' | 'bodySmall' | 'labelLarge' | 'labelMedium' | 'labelSmall'` ‚Äî Default: `'bodyMedium'`. MD3 type scale role. Controls font size, weight, line height, and letter spacing.
- `color?: string` ‚Äî Override the text color. Takes priority over `style.color`. Defaults to the theme's `onSurface` color.
- `style?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Additional text styles. Can override the default theme color via `style.color` when no `color` prop is set.
- `as?: ComponentType<TextProps>` ‚Äî Default: `Text`. Override the underlying text component (e.g. Animated.Text).
- Inherits `TextProps` (except `children`, `style`)

---

### Button

```tsx
import { Button } from '@onlynative/components/button'

<Button variant="filled" leadingIcon="plus" onPress={handlePress}>Create</Button>
<Button variant="outlined">Cancel</Button>
<Button variant="tonal" containerColor="#E8DEF8" contentColor="#1D192B">Custom</Button>
```

Props:
- `children: string` ‚Äî Text label rendered inside the button.
- `variant?: 'filled' | 'elevated' | 'outlined' | 'text' | 'tonal'` ‚Äî Default: `'filled'`. Visual variant. Controls background, border, and text color.
- `leadingIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon rendered before the label. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `trailingIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon rendered after the label. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `iconSize?: number` ‚Äî Default: `18`. Size of leading and trailing icons in dp. Used when resolving string icon names or invoking the render-function form. Pre-rendered elements are not resized.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (label and icon) color. State-layer colors are derived automatically when no containerColor is set.
- `labelStyle?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Additional style applied to the label text.
- Inherits `PressableProps` (except `children`)

---

### IconButton

```tsx
import { IconButton } from '@onlynative/components/icon-button'

<IconButton icon="heart" variant="filled" accessibilityLabel="Like" onPress={handleLike} />
<IconButton icon="heart-outline" selectedIcon="heart" selected={liked} variant="tonal" accessibilityLabel="Like" onPress={toggle} />
```

Props:
- `icon: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon to display. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `selectedIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon to display when `selected` is `true` (toggle mode).
- `iconColor?: string` ‚Äî Overrides the automatic icon color derived from the variant and state.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (icon) color. Takes precedence over `iconColor` when both are provided.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `style?: PressableProps['style']` ‚Äî Custom style applied to the root container.
- `onPress?: () => void` ‚Äî Called when the button is pressed.
- `disabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Disables the button.
- `variant?: 'filled' | 'tonal' | 'outlined' | 'standard'` ‚Äî Default: `'filled'`. Visual style variant.
- `selected?: boolean` ‚Äî Enables toggle mode. The button changes appearance based on selected/unselected state.
- `size?: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large'` ‚Äî Default: `'medium'`. Physical size of the touch target and icon container.
- `accessibilityLabel: string` ‚Äî Required ‚Äî icon-only buttons must have a label for screen readers.

---

### AppBar

```tsx
import { AppBar } from '@onlynative/components/appbar'

<AppBar title="Home" variant="small" />
<AppBar title="Details" canGoBack onBackPress={router.back} insetTop />
<AppBar title="Settings" variant="center-aligned" actions={[
  { icon: 'magnify', accessibilityLabel: 'Search', onPress: onSearch },
  { icon: 'dots-vertical', accessibilityLabel: 'More', onPress: onMore },
]} />
```

Props:
- `title: string` ‚Äî Title text displayed in the bar.
- `variant?: 'small' | 'center-aligned' | 'medium' | 'large'` ‚Äî Default: `'small'`. Layout variant.
- `colorScheme?: 'surface' | 'surfaceContainerLowest' | 'surfaceContainerLow' | 'surfaceContainer' | 'surfaceContainerHigh' | 'surfaceContainerHighest' | 'primary' | 'primaryContainer'` ‚Äî Default: `'surface'`. Color scheme that determines the default container and content colors. `containerColor` and `contentColor` props override these defaults.
- `canGoBack?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When `true`, renders a back button in the leading slot.
- `onBackPress?: () => void` ‚Äî Called when the auto-rendered back button is pressed.
- `insetTop?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When `true`, wraps the bar in a SafeAreaView that handles the top inset.
- `elevated?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When `true`, adds shadow/elevation to indicate the bar is scrolled.
- `leading?: ReactNode` ‚Äî Custom leading content. When provided, overrides `canGoBack`.
- `trailing?: ReactNode` ‚Äî Custom trailing content. When provided, overrides `actions`.
- `actions?: AppBarAction[]` ‚Äî Array of icon-button actions rendered in the trailing slot.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. Applied to both normal and elevated states.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (title and icon) color.
- `titleStyle?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Additional style applied to the title text.
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>` ‚Äî Custom style applied to the root container.

AppBarAction:
- `icon: string` ‚Äî Icon to render. Accepts the same forms as `IconButton.icon` ‚Äî a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function.
- `accessibilityLabel: string` ‚Äî Accessibility label for screen readers (required).
- `onPress?: () => void` ‚Äî Called when the action icon is pressed.
- `disabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Disables the action icon.

---

### Card

```tsx
import { Card } from '@onlynative/components/card'

<Card variant="elevated">{children}</Card>
<Card variant="outlined" onPress={handlePress}>{children}</Card>
```

Props:
- `children: ReactNode` ‚Äî Content rendered inside the card surface.
- `variant?: 'elevated' | 'filled' | 'outlined'` ‚Äî Default: `'elevated'`. Surface style variant.
- `onPress?: () => void` ‚Äî When provided, the card becomes interactive (Pressable). Omit to render as a plain View.
- `disabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Disables the press interaction and reduces opacity. Only effective when `onPress` is provided.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- Inherits `ViewProps`

---

### Chip

```tsx
import { Chip } from '@onlynative/components/chip'

<Chip variant="assist" leadingIcon="calendar">Today</Chip>
<Chip variant="filter" selected={isSelected} onPress={toggle}>Active</Chip>
<Chip variant="input" avatar={<Avatar />} onClose={handleRemove}>John</Chip>
<Chip variant="suggestion" onPress={handleSuggest}>Try this</Chip>
```

Props:
- `children: string` ‚Äî Text label rendered inside the chip.
- `variant?: 'assist' | 'filter' | 'input' | 'suggestion'` ‚Äî Default: `'assist'`. Chip type variant. Controls appearance, allowed interactions, and icon behavior.
- `elevated?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Whether the chip uses an elevated surface instead of an outline border. Available on `assist`, `filter`, and `suggestion` variants. Ignored on `input` variant (always outlined).
- `selected?: boolean` ‚Äî Whether the chip is in a selected (toggled-on) state. Only meaningful for the `filter` variant. Ignored by other variants.
- `leadingIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon rendered before the label. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `iconSize?: number` ‚Äî Default: `18`. Size of the leading icon in dp.
- `avatar?: ReactNode` ‚Äî Custom avatar content (e.g. a small Image or View) to render before the label. Only applicable to the `input` variant. Takes precedence over `leadingIcon`.
- `onClose?: () => void` ‚Äî Callback fired when the close/remove icon is pressed. When provided, renders a trailing close icon. Only renders on `input` and `filter` (when selected) variants.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (label and icon) color. State-layer colors are derived automatically when no containerColor is set.
- `labelStyle?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Additional style applied to the label text.
- Inherits `PressableProps` (except `children`)

---

### Checkbox

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from '@onlynative/components/checkbox'

<Checkbox value={checked} onValueChange={setChecked} />
```

Props:
- `value?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Whether the checkbox is checked.
- `onValueChange?: (value: boolean) => void` ‚Äî Callback fired when the checkbox is toggled. Receives the new value.
- `checkIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Default: `'check'`. Icon shown when the checkbox is checked. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`. Override this when your `iconResolver` doesn't map the default `'check'` name (e.g. a Lucide-only resolver).
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (box) color when checked. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the checkmark icon color.
- Inherits `PressableProps` (except `children`)

---

### Radio

```tsx
import { Radio } from '@onlynative/components/radio'

<Radio value={selected} onValueChange={setSelected} />
```

Props:
- `value?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Whether the radio button is selected.
- `onValueChange?: (value: boolean) => void` ‚Äî Callback fired when the radio is pressed. Receives the new value.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the outer ring and inner dot color when selected. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the outer ring color when unselected.
- Inherits `PressableProps` (except `children`)

---

### Switch

```tsx
import { Switch } from '@onlynative/components/switch'

<Switch value={on} onValueChange={setOn} />
<Switch value={on} onValueChange={setOn} selectedIcon="check" unselectedIcon="close" />
```

Props:
- `value?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Whether the switch is toggled on.
- `onValueChange?: (value: boolean) => void` ‚Äî Callback fired when the switch is toggled. Receives the new value.
- `selectedIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Default: `'check'`. Icon shown on the thumb when selected. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `unselectedIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon shown on the thumb when unselected. When provided, the thumb renders at the larger selected size. Same accepted forms as `selectedIcon`.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the track color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the thumb and icon color.
- Inherits `PressableProps` (except `children`)

---

### TextField

```tsx
import { TextField } from '@onlynative/components/text-field'

<TextField label="Email" variant="outlined" value={email} onChangeText={setEmail} />
<TextField label="Password" variant="filled" error errorText="Required" />
<TextField label="Search" leadingIcon="magnify" trailingIcon="close" onTrailingIconPress={clear} />
```

Props:
- `label?: string` ‚Äî Floating label text. Animates above the input when focused or filled.
- `variant?: 'filled' | 'outlined'` ‚Äî Default: `'filled'`. Container style.
- `supportingText?: string` ‚Äî Helper text displayed below the field. Hidden when `error` or `errorText` is active.
- `errorText?: string` ‚Äî Error message. When provided, implicitly sets `error` to `true` and replaces `supportingText`.
- `error?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When `true`, renders the field in error state with error colors.
- `disabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Disables text input and reduces opacity.
- `leadingIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon rendered at the start of the field. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`.
- `trailingIcon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon rendered at the end of the field. Same accepted forms as `leadingIcon`.
- `onTrailingIconPress?: () => void` ‚Äî Called when the trailing icon is pressed.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. Disabled state still uses the standard disabled appearance.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (input text and icon) color. Error and disabled states take precedence.
- `inputStyle?: StyleProp<TextStyle>` ‚Äî Additional style applied to the text input element.

---

### Layout Components

#### Layout

Full-screen safe area wrapper.

```tsx
import { Layout } from '@onlynative/components/layout'

<Layout>{children}</Layout>
<Layout immersive>{/* No safe area insets */}</Layout>
<Layout edges={['top', 'bottom']}>{children}</Layout>
```

Props:
- `immersive?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When `true`, removes all safe area insets for full-screen layout.
- `edges?: Edge[]` ‚Äî Explicit set of safe-area edges to apply. Overrides `immersive` when provided.
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>` ‚Äî Additional styles applied to the SafeAreaView container.
- Inherits `PropsWithChildren`

#### Box

Base layout primitive with spacing shorthand props.

```tsx
import { Box } from '@onlynative/components/layout'

<Box p="md" bg={theme.colors.surface}>{children}</Box>
<Box px="lg" py="sm" gap="md">{children}</Box>
```

Props:
- `p?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Padding on all sides
- `px?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Horizontal padding (paddingStart + paddingEnd)
- `py?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Vertical padding (paddingTop + paddingBottom)
- `pt?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Padding top
- `pb?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Padding bottom
- `ps?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Padding start (left in LTR, right in RTL)
- `pe?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Padding end (right in LTR, left in RTL)
- `m?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Margin on all sides
- `mx?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Horizontal margin (marginStart + marginEnd)
- `my?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Vertical margin (marginTop + marginBottom)
- `mt?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Margin top
- `mb?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Margin bottom
- `ms?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Margin start
- `me?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Margin end
- `gap?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Gap between children
- `rowGap?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Row gap between children
- `columnGap?: 'xs' | 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl' | number` ‚Äî Column gap between children
- `flex?: number` ‚Äî Flex value
- `align?: FlexAlignType` ‚Äî Align items along the cross axis
- `bg?: string` ‚Äî Background color
- Inherits `ViewProps`

#### Row

Horizontal flex container (extends Box).

```tsx
import { Row } from '@onlynative/components/layout'

<Row gap="sm" align="center">{children}</Row>
<Row wrap>{/* Wraps to next line */}</Row>
```

Props:
- `wrap?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Whether children should wrap to the next line when they overflow.
- `inverted?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Reverses the layout direction (`row-reverse`).

#### Column

Vertical flex container (extends Box).

```tsx
import { Column } from '@onlynative/components/layout'

<Column gap="md">{children}</Column>
```

Props:
- `inverted?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Reverses the layout direction (`column-reverse`).

#### Grid

Equal-width column grid (extends Row).

```tsx
import { Grid } from '@onlynative/components/layout'

<Grid columns={3} gap="sm">{children}</Grid>
```

Props:
- `columns: number` ‚Äî Number of equal-width columns.


---

### List

```tsx
import { List, ListItem, ListDivider } from '@onlynative/components/list'

<List>
  <ListItem headlineText="Title" supportingText="Subtitle" onPress={handlePress} />
  <ListDivider />
  <ListItem
    headlineText="With Icon"
    leadingContent={<Icon name="star" />}
    trailingContent={<Switch value={on} onValueChange={setOn} />}
  />
</List>
```

#### List

Container for list items.

Props:
- `children: ReactNode` ‚Äî Content rendered inside the list container.
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>`
- Inherits `ViewProps`

#### ListItem

Props:
- `headlineText: string` ‚Äî Primary text displayed on the list item.
- `supportingText?: string` ‚Äî Secondary text displayed below the headline.
- `overlineText?: string` ‚Äî Text displayed above the headline (e.g. category label).
- `trailingSupportingText?: string` ‚Äî Short text displayed at the trailing edge (e.g. "100+", timestamp).
- `leadingContent?: ReactNode` ‚Äî Content rendered before the text block (icon, avatar, image, checkbox).
- `trailingContent?: ReactNode` ‚Äî Content rendered after the text block (icon, switch, checkbox).
- `onPress?: () => void` ‚Äî When provided, the item becomes interactive (Pressable). Omit to render as a plain View.
- `disabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. Disables the press interaction and reduces opacity. Only effective when `onPress` is provided.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically.
- `supportingTextNumberOfLines?: number` ‚Äî Default: `1`. Maximum number of lines for supportingText before truncating.
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>`
- Inherits `ViewProps`

#### ListDivider

Props:
- `inset?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `false`. When true, adds a leading inset so the divider aligns with text that follows a leading icon (56dp from the start edge).
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>`
- Inherits `ViewProps`

---

### KeyboardAvoidingWrapper

```tsx
import { KeyboardAvoidingWrapper } from '@onlynative/components/keyboard-avoiding-wrapper'

<KeyboardAvoidingWrapper>
  <TextField label="Name" />
  <TextField label="Email" />
</KeyboardAvoidingWrapper>
```

Props:
- `behavior?: 'padding' | 'height' | 'position'` ‚Äî Default: `'padding'`. Keyboard avoidance strategy.
- `keyboardVerticalOffset?: number` ‚Äî Default: `0`. Extra offset added to the keyboard height calculation. Useful for accounting for headers or tab bars.
- `enabled?: boolean` ‚Äî Default: `true`. Enable or disable the keyboard avoiding behavior.
- `scrollViewProps?: ScrollViewProps` ‚Äî Props forwarded to the inner `ScrollView`.
- `onKeyboardShow?: (event: KeyboardEvent) => void` ‚Äî Called when the keyboard is about to show (iOS) or has shown (Android).
- `onKeyboardHide?: (event: KeyboardEvent) => void` ‚Äî Called when the keyboard is about to hide (iOS) or has hidden (Android).
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>` ‚Äî Style applied to the outer `KeyboardAvoidingView`.
- `contentContainerStyle?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>` ‚Äî Style applied to the inner `ScrollView` contentContainerStyle.
- Inherits `PropsWithChildren`

---

### Avatar

```tsx
import { Avatar } from '@onlynative/components/avatar'

<Avatar imageUri="https://example.com/photo.jpg" size="large" />
<Avatar icon="account" size="medium" containerColor="#E8DEF8" />
<Avatar label="JD" size="small" />
<Avatar icon="plus" onPress={handleAdd} accessibilityLabel="Add user" />
```

Props:
- `imageUri?: string` ‚Äî URI of the image to display. Takes priority over `icon` and `label`.
- `icon?: IconSource` ‚Äî Icon to display. Accepts a string name (resolved via the theme's `iconResolver`, defaulting to `MaterialCommunityIcons`), a pre-rendered element, or a render function that receives `{ size, color }`. Takes priority over `label` when `imageUri` is not set.
- `label?: string` ‚Äî Text initials to display (1‚Äì2 characters recommended). Shown when `imageUri` and `icon` are not set.
- `size?: 'xSmall' | 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' | 'xLarge'` ‚Äî Default: `'medium'`. Size of the avatar.
- `containerColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the container (background) color. State-layer colors (hover, press) are derived automatically when `onPress` is set.
- `contentColor?: string` ‚Äî Override the content (icon / initials) color.
- `style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>` ‚Äî Custom style applied to the root container.
- `onPress?: () => void` ‚Äî When provided, the avatar becomes interactive (Pressable).
- `accessibilityLabel?: string` ‚Äî Required when `onPress` is provided ‚Äî labels the button for screen readers.
- Inherits `ViewProps` (except `style`)


---

## Icons

Every icon prop accepts an `IconSource` (`import type { IconSource } from '@onlynative/utils'`) ‚Äî one of three forms:

1. **String name** ‚Äî resolved through the theme's `iconResolver`. Defaults to `MaterialCommunityIcons` from `@expo/vector-icons`. Browse names at https://pictogrammers.com/library/mdi/.
2. **ReactElement** ‚Äî a pre-rendered icon (`leadingIcon={<Check size={18} color="#fff" />}`). The component does not override size/color.
3. **Render function** ‚Äî `(props: { size: number; color?: string }) => ReactNode`. Receives the component's resolved icon size and color, so the icon stays consistent with theme/variant state.

To swap the default icon library globally, register an `iconResolver` on `ThemeProvider`:

```tsx
import { ThemeProvider } from '@onlynative/core'
import type { IconResolver } from '@onlynative/core'
import { Check, Heart } from 'lucide-react-native'

const icons = { check: Check, heart: Heart }

const lucide: IconResolver = (name, { size, color }) => {
  const Icon = icons[name as keyof typeof icons]
  return Icon ? <Icon size={size} color={color} /> : null
}

<ThemeProvider iconResolver={lucide}>{/* string icon names route to Lucide */}</ThemeProvider>
```

Per-call elements/functions always take precedence over the resolver. `@expo/vector-icons` is only required if you actually pass string icon names without a custom resolver.

---

## Code style

- No semicolons, single quotes, trailing commas
- TypeScript strict mode
- Subpath imports preferred: `@onlynative/components/button` over `@onlynative/components`
